projectDescription: A Chrome MV3 extension that blocks unwanted network traffic using the declarativeNetRequest API, built with Vue 3 and TypeScript.
keyFeatures: Block URLs via wildcard patterns using Chrome declarativeNetRequest API
Optional initiator domain filtering (block only from or exclude specific domains)
Toggle all blocking on/off with a single switch
Inline rule editing via modal with URL filter, initiator domains, and excluded domains
Search and filter rules by URL pattern or domain
Reorder rules with up/down buttons to control matching priority
Duplicate detection warns when adding an existing rule
5-second undo window after deleting a rule
Clear all rules with one click
Export/Import rules as JSON with merge or replace options
Import blocklists directly from a remote URL
Built-in preset blocklists: Advertising, Trackers, Social Media, Malware
Blocked traffic counter with reset capability
Dark mode toggle with persistent preference
publicApiSurface: chrome.storage.local — keys: settings ({ blocked: number, darkMode?: boolean }), blocker ({ rules: Rule[], isEnabled: boolean })
chrome.runtime.sendMessage({ updateRules: { data: Rule[], isEnabled: boolean } }) — update rules from popup to background
Rule schema: { id: number, priority: number, action: { type: 'block' }, condition: { urlFilter: string, initiatorDomains?: string[], excludedInitiatorDomains?: string[] } }
Export format: { rules: Rule[], isEnabled: boolean }
npm run dev — start Vite dev server
npm run build — TypeScript check + production build to dist/
npm run preview — preview production build
npm run package — build + create Chrome Web Store zip
npm run clean — remove dist/ and build/ folders
